text.title: AI Cost Attribution Schema Procedure
text.summary: Six-step procedure from feature list to a reconciled cost dashboard.

step.name: enumerate-features
step.action: List every feature that calls an LLM and the route(s) exposing it. Assign a stable feature slug.

step.name: define-schema
step.action: Write attribution.schema.json with the 11 required fields. Add tenant_id discovery path (auth context, anonymous fallback).
step.decision-gate: Schema must be the single source-of-truth referenced by middleware + aggregator.

step.name: wire-middleware
step.action: Add middleware that wraps every LLM client call: reads tenant_id + feature + route from context, stamps tags, computes cost via pricing snapshot, emits one record per call.
step.decision-gate: Unit test: middleware-wrapped call vs raw call — wrapped must always emit a valid record.

step.name: ingest
step.action: Ship records via the existing pipeline (Kinesis, Kafka, OTel, direct INSERT, etc.). One destination table.
step.decision-gate: Acceptance check: schema validator runs on a 1000-record sample; 100% pass.

step.name: aggregate
step.action: Daily SQL aggregator (templates/daily-aggregator.sql) writes daily_attribution(date, tenant_id, feature, model, calls, tokens, cost_usd). Schedule via cron / Airflow / dbt.
step.decision-gate: Compare sum(cost_usd) for the previous month to the vendor invoice; reconciliation gap should be <5%.

step.name: dashboard
step.action: Build a dashboard with: top tenants by cost, top features by cost, prompt-cache-hit %, model mix, invoice reconciliation card.
step.decision-gate: Quarterly review: any "unknown" segment >1% triggers schema audit.
